terms.definition: A place where people go to buy and sell goods. Markets can be permanent shops or temporary setups like weekly haats.
terms.exampleUsage: We go to the market every Sunday to buy fresh vegetables.

terms.definition: A weekly market set up in an open area on a fixed day. Farmers and small traders sell their goods directly to buyers at a haat.
terms.exampleUsage: The village haat is held every Wednesday near the temple.

terms.definition: Buying or selling goods in very large quantities, usually at a lower price. Shopkeepers buy wholesale and sell in smaller amounts.
terms.exampleUsage: The shopkeeper buys rice wholesale from the mandi and sells it by the kilo.

terms.definition: Selling goods in small quantities directly to customers. The local kirana shop is a retail shop.
terms.exampleUsage: We buy groceries from the retail shop near our house.

terms.definition: Maximum Retail Price - the highest price that can be charged for a packaged product. It is printed on the package by law.
terms.exampleUsage: No shopkeeper can sell a product above its MRP.

terms.definition: Discussing or negotiating the price of something to get a better deal. Bargaining is common in Indian markets and haats.
terms.exampleUsage: My mother is very good at bargaining at the vegetable market.

terms.definition: A large wholesale market, especially for fruits, vegetables, and grains. Farmers bring their produce to sell at mandis.
terms.exampleUsage: Farmers from nearby villages bring their tomatoes to the mandi early in the morning.

terms.definition: A person who buys goods from the producer (farmer) and sells them to shopkeepers, earning a profit in between.
terms.exampleUsage: The middleman bought mangoes cheaply from farmers and sold them at a higher price to shops.

terms.definition: The date printed on a food or medicine package after which it is no longer safe to use.
terms.exampleUsage: Always check the expiry date on milk packets before buying them.

terms.definition: A person who buys and uses goods and services. When you buy something from a shop, you are a consumer.
terms.exampleUsage: As a consumer, you have the right to get good quality products.
